jokes.text: This game is never gonna happen. Half of these guys are still in their cuffs.
jokes.setup: Prison softball game being organized
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that players are literally in handcuffs
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and absurdist logic - treats handcuffed softball as merely inconvenient rather than impossible

jokes.text: I'm catcher, or I'm nothing!
jokes.setup: Michael trying to discuss serious business, George focused on softball position
jokes.punchline: George's dramatic declaration about catching position
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - George's misplaced priorities shown through sports metaphor

jokes.text: Your wife's on line one. Michael's wife had been dead for two years.
jokes.setup: Normal office greeting about wife calling
jokes.punchline: Narrator's matter-of-fact revelation about dead wife
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection with dark comedy - the casual delivery makes the shock funnier

jokes.text: But not immediately.
jokes.setup: Kitty realizes her mistake about the dead wife
jokes.punchline: Narrator's dry commentary on her delayed reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and understatement - narrator's dry commentary on character stupidity

jokes.text: That's actually not any of your business. My business. That's not any of my business.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about company documentation
jokes.punchline: Kitty's confused wordplay about business/business
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay showing Kitty's confusion about corporate hierarchy

jokes.text: If I wanted something your thumb touched, I'd eat the inside of your ear!
jokes.setup: Lucille being turned away from club dining room
jokes.punchline: Bizarre insult about thumb-touching and ear-eating
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Lucille insult - grotesque imagery delivered with aristocratic disdain

jokes.text: You won't even know I was here. But after a shiv to his side... thus fulfilling, in Gob's mind, his contract with the public. Ta-dah?
jokes.setup: Gob's confident claim about escaping undetected
jokes.punchline: Getting stabbed counts as escape success in his mind
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Gob's delusional spin on obvious failure with hesitant 'ta-dah'

jokes.text: I wish I would've known you were coming. I am a mess. Don't know if a call from me would've changed that.
jokes.setup: Kitty apologizing for being a mess
jokes.punchline: Michael's dry observation about her general state
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery - Michael's polite way of saying she's always a mess

jokes.text: Can we come? Sorry. It's not for kids.
jokes.setup: Parents saying they're going for ice cream
jokes.punchline: Ice cream 'not for kids' - clearly lying about therapy
jokes.explanation: Sharp irony - absurd claim that ice cream isn't for children reveals obvious deception

jokes.text: It says 'Bluth Company' right on the side. Plus, it's a stairway. That's gonna catch the eye.
jokes.setup: Kids trying to follow parents inconspicuously
jokes.punchline: Their 'stealth' vehicle is obviously conspicuous
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup - the absurdity of using branded airport equipment for surveillance

jokes.text: Also, that House of Pies that went out of business.
jokes.setup: Narrator describing Bluths' former wealth vestiges
jokes.punchline: Random mention of failed pie restaurant
jokes.explanation: Typical AD throwaway detail - random business failure adds to family's decline narrative

jokes.text: In fact, he'd been terrified of the very notion of prison since, as a young boy, he accidentally watched HBO's Oz mistaking it for the classic Judy Garland musical.
jokes.setup: George Michael's fear of prison
jokes.punchline: Traumatized by wrong 'Oz' - HBO prison drama vs. musical
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - child expecting musical gets brutal prison drama, explains irrational fear

jokes.text: I'm an innocent man! Eat it, bitch!
jokes.setup: Young George Michael expecting Wizard of Oz
jokes.punchline: Violent HBO prison dialogue instead of musical
jokes.explanation: Effective contrast between child's expectations and brutal reality of wrong show

jokes.text: Oh. Wait, wait. Let down your hair. No. Glasses on. Hair back up. Let's just get that hair right back up.
jokes.setup: Gob trying to make Kitty attractive
jokes.punchline: Each change makes her worse, panicked reversal
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy escalation - each 'improvement' makes things worse, Gob's growing horror

jokes.text: Do you want me to turn all the lights off? Yes, yes, please. How's that? Is that better? There's still light coming in from under the door.
jokes.setup: Gob trying to make Kitty less visible
jokes.punchline: Even total darkness isn't dark enough
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of desperation - Gob's standards dropping to absolute darkness

jokes.text: If I gave a performance that good, I'd have my own Alias type show.
jokes.setup: Tobias commenting on Lindsay's emotional honesty
jokes.punchline: He thinks genuine emotion is 'acting' worthy of TV show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias character moment - missing real emotion while praising 'performance'

jokes.text: It's like a carnival. Without the half-person on the skateboard that grabbed your knee to steady himself.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming prison isn't scary
jokes.punchline: Bizarre carnival comparison with disturbing specific detail
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and disturbing image - Michael's attempt to downplay prison backfires

jokes.text: No touching! No touching! No touching!
jokes.setup: George Sr. grabbing at George Michael's hair
jokes.punchline: Prison rule being frantically invoked
jokes.explanation: Classic AD running gag - prison visiting rule becomes catchphrase in moment of panic

jokes.text: No batter. No batter. No batter.
jokes.setup: Prison softball game continues
jokes.punchline: Inmates doing standard baseball chatter amid chaos
jokes.explanation: Mild irony - normal baseball behavior continuing despite family drama

jokes.text: You remember the carjacker? Well, she's certainly toughened up since then.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Lucille being easily threatened
jokes.punchline: Reference to Lucille's previous traumatic experience, she's 'toughened up'
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure - references previous trauma while showing character growth

jokes.text: Daddy horny, Michael.
jokes.setup: George Sr. explaining his need for personal contact
jokes.punchline: Blunt, childlike expression of sexual frustration to his son
jokes.explanation: Legendary moment - perfect economy, maximum cringe, became cultural touchstone. Exemplifies show's commitment to uncomfortable family dynamics.

jokes.text: Pool food. My system's not used to curly fries.
jokes.setup: Lucille making digestive noises
jokes.punchline: Blames aristocratic stomach on lowly pool food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - even her digestion is too refined for regular food

jokes.text: Golf membership want it?
jokes.setup: Lucille agreeing to conjugal visit for George
jokes.punchline: Reveals her true motivation is golf club privileges
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille character moment - reducing intimate gesture to transactional benefit

jokes.text: If I'm no Schwimmer, you're no Jennifer Aniston! Maybe I'm not even Lisa Kudrow.
jokes.setup: Tobias and Lindsay arguing about attractiveness during therapy
jokes.punchline: Comparative insults using Friends cast, Lindsay's self-deprecating escalation
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture comedy showing couple's mutual delusion about their attractiveness

jokes.text: Now there are two men I wanna leave.
jokes.setup: Therapy session revealing relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Lindsay wants to leave both husband and therapist
jokes.explanation: Sharp one-liner with great economy - therapy backfiring spectacularly

jokes.text: I just came from convincing Mom to have sex with Dad. God! What is wrong with you? That is disgusting!
jokes.setup: Michael sharing his 'great news' with Gob
jokes.punchline: Gob's horrified reaction to family intimacy arrangement
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - normal family reaction to Michael's extreme boundary crossing

jokes.text: I Kitty.
jokes.setup: Gob reporting on his mission
jokes.punchline: Using Kitty's name as verb for sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay showing Gob's crude approach to reporting sexual conquest

jokes.text: I'm gonna use the touch lamp to set the mood in the conjugal trailer when Dad's nailing Mom!
jokes.setup: Michael threatening to take back the touch lamp from Gob
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of parents' conjugal visit plans
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation of family discomfort - Michael's revenge through disturbing imagery

jokes.text: Sometimes I don't know if it's gonna be a long or short hug or a middle-medium hug. It's hard to tell sometimes. That was a good hug. That's plenty for now. Hug's gotta end sometime, obviously.
jokes.setup: George Michael and Maeby having awkward hug
jokes.punchline: Over-analyzing the duration and quality of cousin hug
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Michael awkwardness - overthinking simple physical affection with cousin he likes

jokes.text: Every Friday for the last eight years.
jokes.setup: George claiming this is first time with Kitty
jokes.punchline: Reveals long-term affair pattern
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - transforms momentary weakness into systematic betrayal

jokes.text: lit by nothing but natural light.
jokes.setup: Gob describing his strongly worded letter
jokes.punchline: Unnecessary detail about lighting conditions
jokes.explanation: Good Gob moment - adding pretentious detail to mundane letter-writing

jokes.text: This bear needs some honey, wife.
jokes.setup: George's bear metaphor for Lucille
jokes.punchline: Sexual metaphor using bear/honey relationship
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to bear metaphor - extending animal comparison to sexual innuendo

jokes.text: Glasses off. Glasses on. Hair up. Glasses off. Down, on. Have we done up, off?
jokes.setup: Gob still trying to make Kitty attractive
jokes.punchline: Systematic confusion about appearance combinations
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation - Gob's methodical approach to impossible task

jokes.text: Oh, God, Mommy! What did you do?
jokes.setup: Gob pressed against trailer window
jokes.punchline: Accidentally witnessing parents' intimate reunion
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy - Gob's traumatic glimpse of parental intimacy while trapped

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Incredibly guilty.
jokes.setup: George Michael worrying about innocent people in prison
jokes.punchline: Michael's casual confirmation of father's guilt
jokes.explanation: Great dark comedy - Michael's matter-of-fact confirmation destroys son's faith in justice

jokes.text: he's, uh... he's guilty. / He is? / Oh, yeah. Incredibly guilty.
jokes.setup: George Michael worried about innocent people in prison
jokes.punchline: Michael's matter-of-fact confirmation that his father is very guilty
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery that perfectly caps the episode's theme about family loyalty vs. truth

jokes.text: There's a good chance that I... that I may... have committed some, um... light... treason.
jokes.setup: George finally coming clean about international accounts
jokes.punchline: Describing treason as 'light' like it's a beverage option
jokes.explanation: Another legendary AD moment - 'light treason' became iconic for its perfect understatement of serious crime. Exemplifies show's genius for casual treatment of extreme situations.
